steps.title: Spot the tempting but flawed reasoning
steps.content: The passage frames a tempting but flawed reasoning: “similarities make it tempting to treat the geochemical properties of the meteorites as ______ those of Mars's interiors, [but] Mars's geology cannot be ascertained based solely on meteorite-sample analyses.” The blank should express the natural inference the researchers warn against.

steps.title: Find what fits the inference
steps.content:
“Proxies for” means representatives or substitutes—meteorites would serve as stand-ins to understand Mars's interior, which captures the tempting reasoning.
The other three either misname what meteorites would represent (contrivances = artificial schemes, catalysts = triggers for change, deterrents = obstacles).
That confirms A is correct.
